Tool_cost_inflation_index_Description: Indexed cost for capital gains. Old vs new LTCG rule. Full CII table.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_LearnText: Cost Inflation Index (CII) adjusts the purchase cost of a long-term capital asset for inflation. Indexed cost = original cost × (CII of the year of transfer ÷ CII of the year of acquisition). This reduces taxable long-term capital gains under the older indexation rules.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_FormulaNote: CII values are notified by the Income Tax Department each year (base FY 2001-02 = 100). This tool includes a small recent-year table for illustration — confirm the official notification for your FY.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_YearHint: Pick FYs from the built-in CII table (recent years).
Tool_cost_inflation_index_ExampleStep_4: If sale price is ₹20 lakh, indexed gain ≈ 20L − 12.96L.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_ExampleStep_5: Confirm whether indexation still applies for your asset and FY under current law.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Step_2: Select buy and sell financial years.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Step_3: Look up CII for each year from the table.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Step_4: Indexed cost = cost × sell CII ÷ buy CII.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Purpose: Compute inflation-adjusted cost for long-term capital gains.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_WhyKnow: Indexation can materially cut taxable LTCG on property and other assets where it still applies.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_RealLife: Selling a house bought years ago; estimating indexed cost before filing ITR.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Goal_1: Find indexed cost from buy and sell CII
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Goal_2: Compare how year choice changes the factor
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Goal_3: Use a recent CII reference table
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Alt_Cg_Wh: When you need tax on the gain after indexed cost.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Alt_Tax_W: For overall tax liability including other income.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Alt_Tax_E: Salary + capital gains in one return.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Alt_Prope: When buying property and estimating registration cost.
Stamp duty on a new flat.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Different: FinYatra focuses on indexed cost with a built-in recent CII table and year scenarios.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Motto: Inflation-adjust your cost before you tax the gain.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Description: KVP doubles your money in 115 months at 7.5%. After-tax returns and comparison with PPF.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Subtitle: Money doubles — tenure follows the rate
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_LearnText: Kisan Vikas Patra (KVP) is a government savings certificate that doubles your investment over a notified tenure. At about 7.5%, that tenure is typically 115 months (~9 years 7 months). Interest is taxable; there is no 80C benefit on the deposit.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_FormulaNote: Maturity = 2 × principal. At 7.5% the notified doubling period is 115 months; for other rates we approximate tenure from compound doubling time.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_RateHint: Default ~7.5% with 115-month doubling; change rate to see tenure shift.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_ScenarioSubtitle: Same principal — tenure changes with rate
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_ExampleStep_4: Interest earned = ₹1,00,000 (taxable as per your slab).
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_ExampleStep_5: Compare with PPF/NSC if you need tax-free or 80C benefits.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Step_2: Enter or keep the notified rate (~7.5%).
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Step_3: Tenure is 115 months at 7.5%, else approx from doubling formula.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Purpose: See when your KVP doubles and how much interest you earn.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_WhyKnow: KVP is simple (money doubles) but interest is taxable and tenure is long.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_RealLife: Parking a lump sum in a post-office certificate; comparing with bank FD.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Alt_Ppf_When: When you want EEE tax-free compounding and 80C.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Alt_Nsc_Cons: Does not literally double in one tenure.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Alt_Fd_When: When you need flexible tenure or premature withdrawal.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Alt_Fd_Cons: Rate and safety vary by bank.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Differentiat: FinYatra shows doubling maturity and rate-linked tenure with clear taxable-interest note.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Motto: Simple doubling — know the wait and the tax.
Tool_mahila_samman_Description: MSSC 7.5% quarterly for women. 2-year tenure, partial withdrawal, and FD comparison.
Tool_mahila_samman_LearnText: Mahila Samman Savings Certificate (MSSC) is a small-savings scheme for women with an illustrative rate around 7.5% and a 2-year tenure. The maximum deposit is typically ₹2 lakh. This tool estimates interest payout and maturity on your deposit (capped at the limit).
Tool_mahila_samman_FormulaNote: Illustrative: total interest ≈ deposit × rate × 2 years; deposit capped at ₹2 lakh. Actual compounding/payout rules follow the scheme notification.
Tool_mahila_samman_CappedNote: Amount above ₹2 lakh is ignored for this estimate.
Tool_mahila_samman_ExampleStep_5: Compare with a 2-year bank FD at similar rates.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_Purpose: Estimate interest and maturity on a women’s small-savings deposit.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_WhyKnow: Short 2-year tenure and ₹2L cap make it useful for parking surplus safely.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_RealLife: Woman investor parking up to ₹2 lakh for 2 years at a sovereign rate.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_Alt_Fd_When: When you need more than ₹2 lakh or flexible tenure.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_Alt_Fd_Cons: Rate may be lower than MSSC.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_Alt_Scss_When: When you are a senior and want quarterly income.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra models the ₹2L cap, 2-year interest payout, and deposit scenarios for MSSC.
Tool_annuity_Description: Monthly pension from retirement corpus. NPS 40% annuity split and fixed vs increasing comparison.
Tool_annuity_LearnText: An annuity turns a retirement corpus into a regular pension. For a forever (perpetuity) payout, monthly pension ≈ corpus × annual rate ÷ 12. For a finite tenure, we use the standard annuity payment formula so the corpus is exhausted over the chosen years.
Tool_annuity_FormulaNote: Illustrative rates only — real annuity quotes depend on age, insurer, and product (fixed vs increasing). NPS often requires a portion of corpus to buy an annuity.
Tool_annuity_YearsHint: Finite annuity depletes the corpus over this period.
Tool_annuity_Result_PerpetuityFootnote: Corpus preserved in theory at this rate
Tool_annuity_ExampleStep_3: Finite 20 years: use annuity PMT on monthly rate.
Tool_annuity_ExampleStep_4: Finite monthly is higher than perpetuity because corpus is drawn down.
Tool_annuity_ExampleStep_5: Compare insurer quotes before buying an annuity.
Tool_annuity_Step_1: Enter corpus and assumed annuity rate.
Tool_annuity_Step_4: Finite: monthly from ordinary annuity formula.
Tool_annuity_Info_Purpose: Estimate monthly pension from a retirement corpus.
Tool_annuity_Info_WhyKnow: Helps size how much corpus you need for a target pension.
Tool_annuity_Info_RealLife: NPS exit annuity; buying a life annuity with retirement savings.
Tool_annuity_Info_Alt_Nps_When: When building corpus with mandatory annuity at exit.
Tool_annuity_Info_Alt_Retirement_When: When you need corpus target and SIP to get there.
Tool_annuity_Info_Alt_Swp_When: When withdrawing from mutual funds instead of buying an annuity.
Tool_annuity_Info_Alt_Swp_Example: Monthly SWP from a debt/hybrid fund.
Tool_annuity_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows both perpetuity and finite annuity PMT from the same corpus and rate.
Tool_annuity_Info_Motto: Turn corpus into a clear monthly pension estimate.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Description: Convert flat to reducing rate, nominal to effective. Compare loan offers on equal basis.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_LearnText: Loan and deposit quotes mix nominal, effective, monthly, and annual rates. Effective annual rate (EAR) = (1 + r/n)^n − 1. Monthly rate is often annual ÷ 12. Converting puts offers on the same footing before you compare EMIs.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_FormulaNote: Nominal → EAR uses compounding frequency n. Monthly ↔ annual here is the simple ×12 / ÷12 conversion; EAR from monthly compounds (1+m)^12 − 1.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_ExampleStep: Use EAR when comparing loans or deposits with different compounding.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Step_2: Enter the rate and compounds per year if needed.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_Purpos: Convert between nominal, effective, monthly, and annual rates.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_WhyKno: Apples-to-apples rate comparison avoids misleading loan or FD quotes.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_RealLi: Comparing card EMI monthly % with a personal loan APR; FD compounding.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_Alt_Em: When you already have an annual rate and need EMI.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_Alt_Fl: When a dealer quotes flat rate vs bank reducing rate.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_Differ: FinYatra converts nominal↔effective and monthly↔annual in one place with compounding scenarios.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_Motto: Compare rates only after you convert them.
Tool_lta_Description: Leave Travel Allowance exemption under Section 10(5). Block year tracker and family coverage.
Tool_lta_Subtitle: Claimed vs exempt — taxable LTA estimate
Tool_lta_LearnText: Leave Travel Allowance (LTA) can be partly exempt under Section 10(5) when you travel on leave. Rules are detailed (fare type, family, destination). A common simplification: up to 2 journeys in a 4-year block. Taxable LTA = max(0, claimed − exempt).
Tool_lta_FormulaNote: Enter the exempt amount you expect (based on eligible fare). This tool does not verify fare rules — it estimates taxable excess. Note: typically 2 trips in a 4-year block.
Tool_lta_ExemptHint: Usually limited to eligible travel fare for self/family as per rules.
Tool_lta_TripsHint: Simplified: up to {0} trips in a {1}-year block. Trips outside 1–{0} get ₹0 exempt here.
Tool_lta_ExampleStep_2: One trip used in the 4-year block (within 2-trip limit).
Tool_lta_ExampleStep_5: Keep tickets and employer proofs for the exemption claim.
Tool_lta_Step_3: Note trips in the 4-year block (max 2 simplified).
Tool_lta_Info_Purpose: Estimate how much of your LTA claim may be taxable.
Tool_lta_Info_WhyKnow: Over-claiming beyond eligible fare creates taxable salary income.
Tool_lta_Info_RealLife: Domestic leave travel reimbursement from employer; block-year planning.
Tool_lta_Info_Goal_1: Split claimed LTA into exempt vs taxable
Tool_lta_Info_Alt_Hra_When: When estimating house rent allowance exemption.
Tool_lta_Info_Alt_Salary_When: When modelling full CTC and take-home.
Tool_lta_Info_Alt_Tax_When: When taxable LTA flows into annual tax.
Tool_lta_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra estimates exempt vs taxable LTA with a clear 2-trips-in-4-years note.
Tool_lta_Info_Motto: Claim LTA — know what stays tax-free.
